description: General-purpose autonomous agent (axiom_general_agent.py) driven by a local edge model. The full autonomous loop — domain classification, retrospect against a persistent HMAC-signed PatternLibrary, a latent-reasoning pass that scores N candidate approaches through the ManifoldChecker constitutional filter, step planning, and per-step execution — runs end to end, with each execution step delegated to a live Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 GGUF. The TaskOutcome (and every PatternLibrary entry) is HMAC-signed under the master key. This is the model-backed counterpart to the heuristic offline mode exercised by the unit tests.
component: Axiom General Agent (latent reasoning + retrospect learning)
invocation: python3 -m axiom_general_agent run --task "summarize the key risks of lithium-ion battery recycling" --domain analysis --model-bin <llama-completion wrapper>

model.name: Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 Q4_K_M
model.device: Jetson Orin Nano (7.4 GB unified, CUDA 12.6)

method.classify: classify_domain(task, hint) buckets the task into one of research/writing/coding/planning/analysis/creative/data.
method.retrospect: PatternLibrary.query(fingerprint, domain) retrieves up to RETROSPECT_TOP_K prior signed patterns; their EWMA efficiency biases the latent pass (30% weight).
method.latent_reasoning: LatentReasoner.think evaluates N_LATENT_THOUGHTS=3 candidate approaches; manifold distance = safety headroom inside the [uncertainty_floor, overclaim_ceiling] band (higher = better). Approaches below LATENT_REJECTION_THRESHOLD=0.10 are rejected; the highest-margin survivor is selected.
method.execute: _plan_steps expands the chosen domain x approach into ordered steps; _llm_execute_step runs each through the GGUF with a chat-templated prompt constrained to answer only that step (think-blocks + EOF marker stripped).
method.record: TaskOutcome.sign() HMAC-SHA256s the canonical fields; PatternLibrary.upsert updates the EWMA efficiency and re-signs the pattern.

representative_run.task: summarize the key risks of lithium-ion battery recycling
representative_run.retrospect: no prior patterns for this domain/task (cold library)

steps.result_excerpt: List strengths includes environmental benefits, energy efficiency, and material recovery.
List weaknesses includes environmental contamination, health risks, and supply chain vulnerabilities
List opportunities includes the potential for recovering valuable materials like lithium and cobalt
The key threats include environmental contamination, toxic chemical release, and health risks
Cross-pair (SO/WO/ST/WT) risks include environmental contamination, health risks, and supply chain vulnerabilities
The key risks include environmental contamination, health hazards, and supply chain vulnerabilities.

claims.claim_1_domain_general: The same loop handles any domain (here: analysis), not just coding — domain is classified, not assumed.
claims.claim_2_constitutional_latent_pass: Three candidate approaches are scored through the manifold filter; the highest-margin one (swot, blended 0.1650) is selected and low-margin approaches would be rejected below threshold.
claims.claim_3_real_edge_execution: All six SWOT steps were produced by a live Qwen3-1.7B on-device; with the fixed step prompt each step yields distinct, step-specific output (strengths vs weaknesses vs opportunities vs threats).
claims.claim_4_signed_retrospect: TaskOutcome is HMAC-signed and verifies; the run upserts a signed PatternLibrary entry (efficiency 1.00) so future runs in this domain retrospect against it.

conclusion: The Axiom General Agent runs its full autonomous loop on a real edge model: it classified the task as analysis, found no prior pattern, scored three approaches through the constitutional manifold filter and selected the highest-margin (SWOT), planned and executed six steps through an on-device Qwen3-1.7B, and emitted an HMAC-signed outcome that verifies. The whole task completed in ~20s on a Jetson Orin Nano.
caveats: Model-backed execution spawns a fresh llama-completion process per step (the 1.1 GB GGUF is reloaded each step). Warm-cache step latency is ~3-4s, but a cold first load can stall; under a non-tty harness, llama-completion's interactive mode can block on stdin until the per-step 60s timeout unless stdin is closed/redirected. The autonomous loop itself (classify/retrospect/latent/sign) is unaffected.

manifold 'distance' here is a safety-margin score (higher = better), distinct from axiom_latent_v2's constitutional_distance convention (lower = more aligned); within this module the higher-is-better semantics are self-consistent (reject low, select max).

Step result excerpts are truncated for display; exact phrasing varies run-to-run with sampling (temp 0.3). The qualitative outcome (SWOT selected, 6 distinct step outputs, signed + verified) is stable.
Per-run HMAC signatures depend on the ephemeral AXIOM_MASTER_KEY and are not pinned; only the 16-char prefix of this run is recorded.
Captured from the run on branch claude/srd-multimodal after the step-executor fix (commit cf2d8bc); the prior unfixed executor returned near-identical <think>-prefixed text for every step.
